_instructions: This file draws the 'Little Bear River' schematic page (Porcupine Reservoir Outlet down to Cutler Terminus).
See public/schematics/README.md for the full field reference and a longer walkthrough - this is the quick version.
This page ends at the same 'Cutler Reservoir' terminus as lower-logan.json (Little Bear River is one of the inflows
collected there, alongside the main Logan River channel and Blacksmith Fork River) - the terminus node here also carries
linkTo back to lower-logan so a reader can jump back to the main system. lower-logan.json's own 'little_bear' node is the
reciprocal link into this page.
NODES: each node is a card on the diagram, listed in the order the water actually flows.
id         - unique within this file. Tributaries/diversions/links reference another node's id via connectsTo.
name       - for kind 'mainstem'/'tributary'/'diversion', must fuzzy-match a live station's display name from
HydroServer/DWRi/USGS. For 'junction'/'link' nodes it is just label text.
kind       - 'mainstem' (a real gauge station on the main channel), 'junction' (an invisible confluence point on the main
channel, no card - where a tributary/diversion attaches), 'tributary' (water flowing IN - only South Fork,
below, is one), 'diversion' (water flowing OUT - every canal/irrigation company on this page is a diversion).
connectsTo - tributary/diversion only here: the id of the junction this attaches to, or another diversion's id to chain
onto (see the Highline Canal and Paradise Canal chains below - each is several DWRi stations along the same
canal).
side       - tributary/diversion only, when connectsTo is set: 'left' or 'right', which side of the main channel to draw
the card on - meant to reflect which bank of the river the station is really on, not just picked to avoid
overlap (the layout engine grows vertical spacing to fit a crowded side instead of moving anything to the
other side - see SchematicView.vue's computeLayout()).
colorGroup - optional; must match a name from waterway_groups or schematic_groups in config/station_config.yaml.
terminus   - mainstem only: marks this node as the system's final endpoint (only 'cutler_reservoir' below sets this).
linkTo     - the schematic page slug to navigate to; adds a 'view full system' button below the card it's on.
description - terminus node only: subtitle text.
There's no separate edges list - the main channel line, every diversion's connector, and flow direction are all derived
automatically from kind + connectsTo + the order nodes appear above.
Every diversion gets its own junction dot, even when two sit right next to each other in real life - see hyrum_canal_junc
and hyrum_irr_junc, which are two separate junctions rather than one shared between both diversions.
East Fork and South Fork are two separate forks that join at 'fork_confluence', not one continuous channel - South Fork is
modeled as a 'tributary' feeding into that junction (same as any other tributary elsewhere in this app), rather than as a
third sequential mainstem station, so the diagram draws it as an actual confluence.
title: Little Bear River
subtitle: Porcupine Reservoir Outlet down to Cutler Terminus
